x-enumeration-literals.name: Regular Savings Account
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A standard savings account that allows customers to deposit money, earn interest, and withdraw funds when needed, usually with some withdrawal limitations.

Comment:
Example:
A customer deposits €3,000 and earns 1% annual interest while still being able to withdraw money through online banking.

x-enumeration-literals.name: High-Interest Savings Account
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A savings account that offers higher interest rates compared to standard savings accounts, often requiring a higher minimum balance.

Comment:
Example:

Minimum balance: €10,000

Interest rate: 3% per year.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Term Deposit
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Term Deposit (Fixed Deposit Account) A savings product where money is locked for a fixed period, and the bank pays a higher interest rate in return.

Comment:
Example:
A customer deposits €5,000 for 12 months at 3.5% interest, payable at maturity.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Notice Savings Account
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A savings account where customers must give advance notice before withdrawing funds.

Comment:
Example:
A 60-day notice account requires the customer to inform the bank 60 days before withdrawing money.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Youth Savings Account
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A savings account specifically designed for children or young adults, often with higher interest rates or savings incentives.

Comment:
Example:
Accounts for customers under 18 offering 2% interest and no maintenance fees.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Retirement Savings Account
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A long-term savings product designed to help customers accumulate funds for retirement, sometimes with tax benefits.

Comment:
Example:
A retirement savings plan where contributions grow until retirement age.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Goal-Based Savings Account
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A savings account designed to help customers save for a specific financial goal.

Comment:
Example:
Saving €10,000 over two years to buy a car, with bonus interest when the goal is achieved.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Online Savings Account
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A savings account managed entirely through digital banking platforms, often offering higher interest rates due to lower operational costs.

Comment:
Example:
Customers open and manage the account through mobile banking and earn higher interest than branch accounts.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Loyalty Savings Account
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A savings account that rewards customers for keeping funds in the account for a long period without withdrawing them.

Comment:
Example:
A loyalty bonus interest paid after maintaining a balance for 12 months.
